rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical anodic decarboxylative lactonisation of allyl hemioxalate salts with aliphatic carboxylic acids to form γ-butyrolactones via radical cyclisation/cross-coupling
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation (Pt) of the hemioxalate salt to an oxycarbonyl radical via decarboxylation, while the cathode (Pt) reduces protons (from MeOH/NH4+/co-acid) generating methoxide that deprotonates the co-acid; both electrodes contribute productively (paired electrolysis)

key_intermediates.name: alkyl radical R• from decarboxylation of co-acid
acyl nitrene/isocyanate from oxamic acid (lactam attempt)
carbamate 20 from methoxide trapping of nitrogen-centred cation

electron_flow: Anode oxidises the hemioxalate to an oxycarbonyl radical that loses CO2 and undergoes 5-exo-trig cyclisation onto the pendant alkene, giving a primary C-radical; in parallel, the co-acid carboxylate is anodically decarboxylated to an alkyl radical, and the two radicals recombine at/near the electrode to give the γ-substituted lactone; the cathode reduces protons to H2, generating methoxide that maintains deprotonation of the co-acid.

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of hemioxalate vs aliphatic acid
probe_reagents.observation: Hemioxalate 1 is easier to oxidise than the aliphatic co-acid, justifying portionwise addition of 1 to maintain low concentration

probe_reagents.name: carbon graphite electrode (control)
probe_reagents.observation: Unsuitable due to high H overpotential and strong adsorption of organics; supports choice of Pt

probe_reagents.name: deoxygenation/drying of solvent
probe_reagents.observation: No impact on yield, consistent with robust radical pathway at electrode surface

probe_reagents.name: N-tosyl hemioxalate 18 (lactam attempt)
probe_reagents.observation: No exo-cyclisation; instead overoxidation to N-centred cation trapped by MeOH/MeO- to give carbamate 20, indicating cation formation outcompetes radical cyclisation for N-substrates

probe_reagents.name: secondary co-acid (→ lactone 7)
probe_reagents.observation: Lower yield consistent with known anodic overoxidation of secondary alkyl radicals to carbocations

probe_reagents.name: halide-bearing co-acid (→ lactone 10)
probe_reagents.observation: Halogenated chain tolerated, indicating short radical lifetime / rapid recombination near electrode (no halide abstraction)

notes: Main by-product is the R-R homodimer from co-acid radicals; excess (5 equiv) co-acid is used to suppress homo-dimerisation of the cyclised radical. Attempted lactam synthesis from oxamic acids fails because nitrogen lone pair stabilises a cation pathway; even with EWG (Ts, OTf) on N, overoxidation dominates and methoxide traps the cation to give carbamate 20 rather than radical cyclisation product 19.
